introSection: Avengers Assemble: Flight Force is a steel launched enclosed roller coaster located at Walt Disney Studios Park in Marne la Vallée, Île-de-France, France. The ride opened on 16 March 2002 as Rock 'n' Roller Coaster and was themed to rock band Aerosmith. It closed on 1 September 2019 for a complete retheme, and reopened with its new Marvel theming on 20 July 2022 at the same time as the new Avengers Campus.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
During the D23 Expo in Japan in February 2018, it was announced that Rock 'n' Roller Coaster would be receiving a remodel, featuring Iron Man theming. The ride would close on 1 September 2019. Guests were given a free magazine in the Fastpass queue on the ride's last day of operation. The entrance sign was taken down in mid-September 2019.

The ride reopened on 20 July 2022, along with the new Avengers Campus area.

New effects were added in 2023, including laser projections that display a starfield.

sections.title: Design
sections.content: Avengers Assemble: Flight Force is an LSM Coaster from Vekoma. It is an exact clone of Rock 'n' Roller Coaster at Disney's Hollywood Studios in Lake Buena Vista, Florida, USA.

sections.title: Station
sections.content: Avengers Assemble: Flight Force's station has separate areas for loading and unloading.

sections.title: Theming
sections.content:
Florida's coaster is themed as a limousine transporting riders to an Aerosmith concert, whereas Paris' was themed to them making an Aerosmith music video. Each train would have its own soundtrack.

Soundtrack 1: Green Lightshow ("Back In The Saddle" and "Dude Looks Like A Lady") Soundtrack 2: Purple Lightshow ("Young Lust", "F.I.N.E.", and "Love In An Elevator") Soundtrack 3: Multicolor Lightshow ("Love In An Elevator" and "Walk This Way") Soundtrack 4: Red Lightshow ("Nine Lives") Soundtrack 5: Blue Lightshow ("Sweet Emotion")

fullText:
Avengers Assemble: Flight Force is a steel launched enclosed roller coaster located at Walt Disney Studios Park in Marne la Vallée, Île-de-France, France. The ride opened on 16 March 2002 as Rock 'n' Roller Coaster and was themed to rock band Aerosmith. It closed on 1 September 2019 for a complete retheme, and reopened with its new Marvel theming on 20 July 2022 at the same time as the new Avengers Campus.

During the D23 Expo in Japan in February 2018, it was announced that Rock 'n' Roller Coaster would be receiving a remodel, featuring Iron Man theming. The ride would close on 1 September 2019. Guests were given a free magazine in the Fastpass queue on the ride's last day of operation. The entrance sign was taken down in mid-September 2019.

The ride reopened on 20 July 2022, along with the new Avengers Campus area.

New effects were added in 2023, including laser projections that display a starfield.

Avengers Assemble: Flight Force is an LSM Coaster from Vekoma. It is an exact clone of Rock 'n' Roller Coaster at Disney's Hollywood Studios in Lake Buena Vista, Florida, USA.

Avengers Assemble: Flight Force's station has separate areas for loading and unloading.

Florida's coaster is themed as a limousine transporting riders to an Aerosmith concert, whereas Paris' was themed to them making an Aerosmith music video. Each train would have its own soundtrack.

Soundtrack 1: Green Lightshow ("Back In The Saddle" and "Dude Looks Like A Lady") Soundtrack 2: Purple Lightshow ("Young Lust", "F.I.N.E.", and "Love In An Elevator") Soundtrack 3: Multicolor Lightshow ("Love In An Elevator" and "Walk This Way") Soundtrack 4: Red Lightshow ("Nine Lives") Soundtrack 5: Blue Lightshow ("Sweet Emotion")
